log.rate_off: [yellow]No rate limit - up to {concurrency} pages at once, as fast as the target replies. Use --rate-limit for production systems.[/yellow]
log.restored: [green]{count} results restored from the previous scan[/green]
log.scan_summary: Result: {matches} OK | {diffs} diffs | {new} new | {errors} errors | {timeouts} timeouts
log.baseline_promoted: [green]{count} screenshots stored as the new reference[/green]
notify.robots_all_blocked: All URLs are disallowed by robots.txt!
notify.no_urls_loaded: No URLs loaded! Please load a sitemap first.
notify.scan_running_reset: A scan is running! Please wait for it to finish.
notify.no_screenshot: No screenshot available! Please scan first.
error.title: Error
about.description:
Detects visual changes on websites by capturing every page of the sitemap and comparing it against a stored reference.

Differences are measured pixel by pixel and reported above a configurable threshold - so unintended changes after a deployment surface before anyone else notices them.
detail.no_selection:
No URL selected.

Pick a URL from the table.
detail.btn.compare_browser: Compare all images in the browser
detail.new_baseline_body:
No previous reference available.
The screenshot was stored as the new reference.
diffscreen.title: Diff details: {url}
history.title: History - sitemaps checked so far
history.empty: No entries yet. After the first run the sitemap shows up here.
reset.title: Reset
reset.message:
All images for {host} will be deleted!

Affected: {count} files (reference, screenshots, diffs)

The sitemap is reloaded afterwards.
scanmode.title: Scan mode
scanmode.intro:
There are already {baseline} reference images and {current} current screenshots.

How should the new scan proceed?
scanmode.a.head: Scan again
scanmode.a.desc1: New screenshots replace the current ones.
scanmode.b.head: Update reference + scan
scanmode.b.desc1: The current screenshots become the new reference.
scanmode.b.desc2: Then a new scan is run.
settings.threshold_hint: From how many percent of changed image area a page counts as a deviation. Smaller values find more, but also report rendering noise (font smoothing, animations).
settings.full_page_check: Whole page instead of just the visible area
settings.full_page_hint: On = the complete page is captured; to do that the page is scrolled through so lazy-loaded content appears. That takes longer and creates more load than capturing the visible area.
settings.rate_hint: On by default. Off = the scanner works as fast as the target replies. "Parallel tabs" only caps concurrency, not the rate. For every screenshot the page is rendered completely in a real browser - scripts, fonts and images included, bypassing the server cache. One page therefore weighs a multiple of a plain request, and a run touches every page of the sitemap.
settings.robots_hint: On = pages disallowed by robots.txt are skipped (the count appears in the log). Off only makes sense for your own systems, e.g. when a test system blocks everything wholesale.
settings.graphics_hint: Needs a restart. Off = the preview is drawn from Unicode half blocks and renders safely on any terminal. On = pixel-exact display, if your terminal speaks Sixel or the Kitty protocol.
settings.cookies_hint: Cookies are set before the capture - useful for protected test systems or to skip a consent banner that would otherwise cover every screenshot.
cli.description: Detects visual changes on websites by comparing screenshots
cli.examples:
Examples:
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml --threshold 0.5
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml --viewport 1280x720
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml --output-html report.html
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml --filter /products
  visual-regression-scanner https://example.com/sitemap.xml --cookie auth=token123

Keyboard shortcuts in the TUI:
  u = enter URL       c = start scan               s = settings
  h = history         r = reset                    R = reports
  o = images in the browser                        l = log on/off
  e = diffs only      i = about                    q = quit

  Copy, export or hide the log: right-click inside the log area.
  The height of the log area can be dragged at the handle above it.
cli.help.screenshots_dir: Root directory for screenshots (default: ./screenshots). One subdirectory per site is created.
cli.help.threshold: Diff threshold in percent (default from the settings: 0.1)
cli.help.concurrency: Maximum parallel browser tabs (default from the settings: 4)
cli.help.rate_limit: Max. pages per minute (default: 60). With 0 the scan runs unthrottled - every page is fully rendered for the screenshot and loads a production system accordingly
cli.help.ignore_robots: Ignore robots.txt (only sensible for your own sites)
cli.help.cookie: Set a cookie (e.g. --cookie auth=token). Can be repeated.
shot.start: Capturing screenshots of {total} URLs (parallel tabs: {concurrency}, timeout: {timeout}s, viewport: {width}x{height})
sitemap.error.retries: The sitemap could not be loaded after {count} attempts: {error}
sitemap.error.parse: The sitemap XML could not be parsed: {error}
